From earliest years Summerside, many women from outlying Acadian communities came here find work.
They were employed truckers, seamen, fishermen, farmers, carpenters, coopers, tinsmiths, stonemasons, maids, dressmakers, washerwomen.
Through years, they advanced more lucrative prestigious occupations.
Among Summerside's first Acadian entrepreneurs were Frank Perry (François Poirier) wife Annie Arsenault.
1899, they opened popular Queen Hotel that served community half century.
This 35-room hotel, described "one most comfortable homelike hotels Maritime Canada," located south Journal-Pioneer next railroad track, today Confederation Trail.
Standing Queen Hotel's steps Aubin Arsenault Joseph Gaudet.
Aubin Arsenault (1870-1968) Summerside's first Acadian lawyer.
also first francophone elected town council where from 1906 until 1908, year elected Legislative Assembly representing Prince.
1917, became Premier province 1921 appointed Supreme Court Prince Edward Island.
Joseph Gaudet (1880-1933), better known "Joe Bunn", born Summerside.
young man, worked trucker then opened local restaurant business.
1908, Gaudet operated first movie theatres Summerside, "The Happyland."
1922, "Joe Bunn" Gaudet opened Capitol Theatre Central Street.
Today, third Summerside's population claims Acadian ancestry.
most common Acadian names city Arsenault, Bernard, Blacquiere, Blanchard, Cormier, DesRoches, Doucette, Gallant, Gaudet, Perry (Poirier), Peters (Pitre), Richard, Sonier, Wedge (Aucoin).

When making HTTP requests pages over Internet, browser sends number information header lines webserver which will serve that request.
Those HTTP information headers only contain information about page that retrieved, also contain configuration system information browser computer browser running
This information used webserver serve best matching content client.
Most Internet users also many website builders aware this extra information which accompagnies every request.
Proper these HTTP headers webservers however improve user experience significantly matching response close possible user browser preferences.
example serving webpages primary language browser.
Another example serving light weight pages when mobile device connecting, send images format best processed that particular browser output device.
Browsers send variety HTTP headers.
This list contains most common HTTP headers will coming from regular browsers bots like crawlers from major search engines.
Accept This HTTP header contains MIME types which accepted browser.
This list specific MIME types, list also contains wildcards.
entries list separated commas.
Priority values assigned each MIME type adding semicolon followed q=val parameter.
priority value ranges from where higher values represent higher priority.
this browser request specific content types, example files over files both available server.

content HTTP header list more languages.
